reason: The text presents the opposite scenario. The Israelite people blame Moses (a leader) for their predicament, complaining that he has taken them out of Egypt to die in the wilderness (Exodus 14:11-12). Moses does not blame the people; instead, he responds by reassuring them and encouraging trust in God's salvation (Exodus 14:13-14): 'Fear not; stand firm, and see the salvation of the Lord.' There is no instance in this passage where Hebrew leaders blame the people for their predicament.
